title: Astrotricha glabra
taxonomy: Astrotricha glabra is classified within the family Asteliaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants, commonly known as the astelia family. Astrotricha glabra is native to New South Wales and Queensland in Australia, where it is found growing in sandy soil. The species is also known as the smooth rat's tail or glabrous rat's tail due to its unique appearance. Astrotricha glabra is further classified within the genus Astrotricha, which includes several species of plants native to Australia and New Zealand.

Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, inflammation, and digestive problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
